Overview Request flow HTTP API Storage Roadmap

Roadmap

v0 is intentionally small. Each item below is a clean layer over the current code — not a rewrite.

Near-term

1. Daemon lifecycle on macOS

Today crated runs in the foreground; if you close the terminal, it dies. A small launchd plist (~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.crate-html.crated.plist) would survive shell sessions and start on login. Optional brew services integration follows naturally.

2. Pi coding agent skill

The Claude Code skill is in .claude/skills/crate-push/SKILL.md. The Pi-side equivalent is the same shape — a manifest that wraps crate push. Same API, different agent.

"Pi" here means the Pi coding agent. A peer to Claude Code, not Raspberry Pi hardware. crate-html has no embedded-hardware story.

3. crate push --open

One-flag shortcut to open the URL in a browser after a successful push. Same as running crate push then crate open, but with one fewer step.

Medium-term

4. TLS via Caddy

If you ever want a real hostname instead of localhost:7777, put Caddy in front:

crate.local {
  reverse_proxy 127.0.0.1:7777
}

No code change in crated. Caddy handles certs, vhosts, HTTP/2, the works.

5. Tailnet exposure

Run crated on the laptop and reach it from any device on your tailnet via the Mac's 100.x.y.z address. Combined with Caddy + a real hostname, this is the "share a draft with your phone" loop.

6. crate logs <site>

Per-site access log tail. Useful to see what an agent (or a human) actually fetched after a push.

Things we are explicitly not doing

Not doingWhy
DatabaseFilesystem is enough. readdir is the index.
Multi-tenant authOne human, one machine. Single bearer token.
Server-side renderingSites are static. If you want SSR, render to static first, then push.
Vhosts in cratedPath routing is enough. Caddy can rewrite if you ever want vhosts.
Embedded hardwarev0 is laptop-only. Agents call into it, not the other way around.

Far future

Open shape, not committed:

  • Diff views. If crate push notices a name already exists, show what changed file-by-file.
  • TTLs. crate push --expires 24h for ephemeral drafts that self-clean.
  • Templates. crate new plan scaffolds a starter site so agents don't have to generate the boring chrome each time.
← storage · back to overview
